$comment: ds.dotui.org tier-1 roster — approved by maintainer 2026-07-03 (see README.md). Moves to ds/data/roster.json when plan 003 lands. Scores: color/token dimension only, 0-3: [technical-depth, originality-lessons, inspectability, docs-quality, influence].

systems.name: Adobe Spectrum 2
systems.rationale: Contrast-solved generation (Leonardo, CAM02-UCS), true 3-tier tokens with light/dark/wireframe modes, JSON-Schema-validated pipeline.

systems.name: Google Material 3
systems.rationale: HCT color space, runtime dynamic color from a seed, contrast-by-construction via tone deltas; open in six language ports.

systems.name: Radix Colors + Themes
systems.rationale: 12-step role semantics the industry imitates; only major APCA-by-construction system (step 11 = Lc 60, step 12 = Lc 90); P3 progressive enhancement. Plan-002 pilot (open method).

systems.name: USWDS
systems.rationale: Luminance-locked color grades: numeric grade difference deterministically guarantees WCAG contrast (the 'magic number' system).

systems.name: Ant Design
systems.rationale: Fully open parametric HSV ramp algorithm plus the seed-to-map-to-alias derivation pipeline (derived non-color tokens too).

systems.name: Microsoft Fluent 2
systems.rationale: Open Lab/LCH curve-based ramp generator (paletteShadesFromCurve) with a public Theme Designer; clean global-to-alias token split; high-contrast theme factory.

systems.name: Shopify Polaris
systems.rationale: Open HSLuv color factory built for cross-hue contrast parity at equal lightness.

systems.name: GitHub Primer
systems.rationale: 3-tier Style Dictionary pipeline (base/functional/component), HSLuv Prism authoring, automated WCAG token-pair audits, org.primer.llm machine-readable extension.

systems.name: Astryx
systems.rationale: Semantic-purpose tokens with no numeric ramps, light-dark() dual-mode values, 10 swappable CSS-variable themes; the strongest counter-model to ramp-based systems. Open since June 2026.

systems.name: Linear
systems.rationale: LCH three-variable theme generation (base/accent/contrast) replacing 98 per-theme variables; semantic token layer extractable from shipped CSS. Plan-002 pilot (reverse-engineered method).

systems.name: Stripe
systems.rationale: Canonical CIELAB contrast methodology (2019 post, still live) plus 284 color custom properties extractable from shipped dashboard CSS — a claimed-vs-shipped study.

systems.name: IBM Carbon
systems.rationale: Mature 3-tier token layering at enterprise scale (~235 semantic tokens, 4 built-in themes); palette hand-picked — architecture lessons, not generation.

systems.name: Vercel Geist
systems.rationale: Step-as-role 100-1000 numbering and the machine-readable design.md spec; maintainer pick for dev-tool relevance despite thin color science.

systems.name: shadcn/ui
systems.rationale: The ecosystem reference point: semantic CSS-variable convention over borrowed static scales, no generation, no contrast strategy — the honest profile is itself a finding.

systems.name: Tailwind CSS palette
systems.rationale: dotUI's substrate: 26x11 curated palette, the v4 hex-to-OKLCH migration preserving cross-hue balance, @theme token namespaces; no semantic layer or contrast strategy — a load-bearing absence.

systems.name: Salesforce Lightning
systems.rationale: Origin of 'design tokens'; SLDS 2 global styling hooks and the hard SLDS1-to-2 break; generation tooling not open. Candidate for a history topic page.

systems.name: Atlassian Design System
systems.rationale: Foundation.Property.Modifier naming grammar and 4-plane elevation model; generation story thin, docs resist inspection.

systems.name: Chakra UI v3
systems.rationale: Hand-picked hex, but the per-palette semantic shape and colorPalette CSS-variable indirection are a clean re-theming pattern.

systems.name: Mantine
systems.rationale: Open HSL fixed-map generator with self-admitted light-hue failure modes plus off-by-default luminance autoContrast — an honest legacy counter-example.

systems.name: Open Props
systems.rationale: Dual generation strategies (composited palettes vs parametric OKLCH single-hue curve); no semantic layer or contrast tooling.

systems.name: GOV.UK Design System
systems.rationale: Accessibility-process exemplar with a functional-colour Sass layer; contrast is developer-verified convention, palette hand-authored.

systems.name: Arco Design
systems.rationale: Targeted look only: hue-band-dependent dark-mode palette algorithm (palette-dark.js); docs unreachable and the color package is stale.

systems.name: Hero UI
systems.rationale: Footnote-level case study: v2-to-v3 abandoned shade scales plus computed contrast for flat OKLCH roles with color-mix() states.

systems.name: Airbnb DLS
systems.rationale: Effectively closed: no public artifacts, site blocks bot inspection, only philosophy pieces and community reconstructions.

systems.name: Nord Design System
systems.rationale: Source repo not public, npm ships compiled output only, usage restricted to Nordhealth products; generation algorithm undisclosed.

systems.name: Orbit
systems.rationale: Thin color/token story; docs unreachable during recon; dedicated tokens repo archived.

systems.name: Uber Base
systems.rationale: Hand-curated hex, Styletron CSS-in-JS theming, no contrast methodology, internal-first posture; little transferable.

systems.name: Park UI
systems.rationale: Curation layer: the color science is Radix's (vendored) and the token mechanics are Panda's — study those instead.

systems.name: Once UI
systems.rationale: The algorithmic-generation impression did not survive source inspection: hardcoded palettes swapped by data-brand attribute, an unfinished custom escape hatch, no contrast code.

systems.name: Untitled UI
systems.rationale: Not slop, but not color science: real 3-layer semantic architecture in an open repo, undermined by plain-RGB values, no generation methodology, and marketing-only WCAG claims. Footnote in the token-layers topic page at most.
